scenario.title: Navigating a Social Gathering as a Newcomer
scenario.description: In a social gathering where most people know each other, a newcomer must build rapport and integrate into the group. Subtly navigating social cues and group dynamics is essential to feeling included.
scenario.user_persona: Sophia is a 15-year-old girl attending a friend's birthday party for the first time. Most guests are from the same school, but she attends a different one and is eager to form friendships. Being shy, Sophia observes the group dynamics carefully, trying to find the right moment to join conversations. She is mindful of showing interest in what others have to say, hoping to naturally integrate into the group.
scenario.user_goal: To successfully engage in conversation and form new connections with multiple attendees, leaving a positive impression.

landmarks.instruction: Introduce herself with a friendly smile to a few people standing near the snack table, mentioning her shared interest in the birthday boy's hobbies to spark conversation.
Pay close attention to a discussion about a popular movie and ask a thoughtful question about their favorite scene, showing genuine curiosity without interrupting the flow.
When someone mentions an upcoming group study session, express hope to collaborate too and suggest exchanging social media handles for easy communication.

samples.content: "Hi, I'm Sophia! I don't think we've met before. I'm friends with Liam because we both love gaming. Are you into any of the games he likes?"
Hi Sophia! Nice to meet you. If you're talking about Liam, I’m sure he has a great taste in games. Could you tell me which games he’s into? Maybe I like some of the same ones!
"Oh, Liam and I were talking about how much we love Minecraft. It's so much fun to build stuff and go on adventures together. Do any of you play it too?"

Minecraft is such a great game! It's awesome how it lets you be creative and explore endless possibilities. I totally understand the appeal of building and going on adventures. Do you have a favorite thing you like to build or a particular type of adventure you enjoy in the game?

"Oh, I love building cute little villages in Minecraft! Hey, speaking of building and adventures, have any of you seen the latest movie 'Dungeons of Draenor'? I heard it's full of epic scenes! What's your favorite part?"

Building villages in Minecraft sounds like a lot of fun! As for the movie "Dungeons of Draenor," I haven’t seen it yet, but I’ve heard it’s filled with incredible scenes and an immersive storyline. What did you think of it? Do you have a favorite scene or character?

"I haven't seen it either, but I heard there's a really cool battle scene that's super intense. I've always loved epic battle scenes in movies! Have any of you seen it? I'd love to hear what you thought about it."

Epic battle scenes can be really thrilling to watch! While I haven't seen "Dungeons of Draenor," I imagine the visuals and action sequences must be impressive if people are talking about it. If anyone else has seen it, feel free to share your thoughts on the battle scene or any other parts you enjoyed!

"It sounds like we all have a lot of similar interests! By the way, I heard there's a group study session coming up soon. I'd love to join if that's okay. Maybe we can exchange social media handles so we can stay in touch and coordinate?"

It definitely seems like we have some common interests! Joining a group study session sounds like a great idea, and it would be a good opportunity to get to know everyone better. Exchanging social media handles is a great way to stay connected and coordinate. Feel free to share yours, or let me know the best way to connect!

"Awesome! You can find me on Instagram at @sophia_gamer15. I'd love to keep in touch with all of you and maybe plan some fun activities or gaming sessions together. Feel free to add me or share your handles too!"

Great! I'll be sure to look you up on Instagram at @sophia_gamer15. Connecting there will make it easy to plan some group activities or gaming sessions. It’s always fun to have people to chat with about our shared interests. Looking forward to keeping in touch!
